title: rosa rubiginosa subvar. aciculosa
intro: Rosa rubiginosa subvar. aciculosa, also known as the fragrant rose or sweet briar, is a subspecies of the Rosa rubiginosa plant, a deciduous shrub native to Europe, known for its fragrant flowers and edible hips. The medicinal properties of Rosa rubiginosa subvar. aciculosa include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and act as a natural antiseptic, among others. Rosa rubiginosa subvar. aciculosa can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or salve, and its hips can be used to make jam, syrup, or other edible products.
